checks.detail: check:release=pnpm typecheck && pnpm check:product-cutover && pnpm check:agent-api && pnpm check:public-api && pnpm check:assets-cli && pnpm check:asset-corpus && pnpm check:agent-docs && pnpm check:templates && pnpm check:examples && pnpm check:devtools && pnpm check:deployment && pnpm check:docs-site && pnpm check:bundle-size && pnpm check:marketing-truth && pnpm dogfood:agent && pnpm check:tarballs && pnpm check:clean-install && pnpm check:docs-codeblocks && pnpm check:marketing-links && pnpm check:error-quality && pnpm check:prompt-fidelity && pnpm check:effects-vfx && pnpm check:product-context && pnpm check:test-plan-status
active template dirs: cinematic-scene, mini-game, product-viewer
pnpm workspace does not include archive paths
root template files: templates/product-viewer, templates/cinematic-scene, templates/mini-game
fresh Codex context-only result is documented
starter-template visual review documents current starter product-quality screenshots and boundary
starter-example visual review documents active example screenshots and product-quality boundary
prompt-to-visual quality boundary is documented with starter pass and broad remaining gaps
prompt-plan API exports and active packaged starters are present
agent API test covers warnings for vague prompt plans
public prompt-facing rain and bloom have renderer-owned Three paths beyond symbolic lines/no-op
effects audit total=25, pass=25, partial=0, fail=0, contactSheet=tests/reports/effects-vfx-visual-audit-contact-sheet.png
7/7 known gaps have owner, next action, and target evidence
37/37 claims have pass evidence; 0/0 claim gaps and 7/7 known gaps are tracked

claims.claim: Aura3D is the editable scene layer for agent-written browser 3D.
AI coding agents write TypeScript or JavaScript against a compact public API.
Users bring their own assets.
claims.nextAction: Asset corpus includes authenticated Sketchfab CC0 download/import/typegen/browser-render proof; Meshy exports remain external.

claims.claim: Aura3D provides typed asset references.
Aura3D provides starter templates.
Starter templates render through WebGL2 and have scene-specific render-plumbing screenshot profile checks.
Aura3D provides diagnostics.
Aura3D provides screenshots.
Aura3D provides static deployment checks.
Public packages work from packed artifacts in clean npm projects.
@aura3d/engine exposes the public engine surface.
@aura3d/react is an optional thin React adapter.
@aura3d/cli supports asset, doctor, deployment, serve, and agent-file flows.
create-aura3d scaffolds product-viewer, cinematic-scene, and mini-game.
Agent-readable context is useful.
claims.nextAction: Codex and Claude Code context evals pass; run Cursor and Copilot separately when available.

claims.claim: A fresh Codex context-only run can build a compiling WebGL2 app with typed assets.
claims.nextAction: Run Cursor and Copilot separately; this only proves a fresh Codex run and not product-quality visual fidelity.

claims.claim: Claude Code can complete the five-task context-only eval from agent context and public tarballs.
claims.nextAction: This is one external-agent pass; Cursor and Copilot remain separate subscription runs.

claims.claim: Codex dogfood uses prompt-plan helpers, typed assets, route health, screenshot profile checks, and product-quality visual review for the deterministic self-test.
Codex five-task context eval completes product viewer, camera/rain, reflective floor, click-swap, and static preview tasks with typed assets and no API hallucinations.
claims.nextAction: This is local Codex evidence only; run the same five-task eval with external agents before claiming cross-agent proof.

claims.claim: Codex repair eval improves a failed screenshot to product-quality by applying prompt-plan repair hints with a recorded repair turn.
claims.nextAction: This is local Codex evidence only; run external agent repair turns separately before claiming broad repair-loop behavior.

claims.claim: The public agent API includes prompt-plan helpers and the three starter templates use that prompt-plan flow.
Prompt-plan reports warn when required visual information is missing from vague plans.
Prompt-facing rain and bloom effects have renderer-owned visual implementations beyond the previous symbolic/no-op path.
claims.nextAction: Keep screenshot regression review on the starter and dogfood routes; this does not prove premium VFX parity.

claims.claim: The effects/VFX surface is audited for visual acceptability instead of assuming named effects are finished.
claims.nextAction: The audit passes at starter/helper level; route-level screenshots and human review are still required for premium VFX claims.

claims.claim: The three release-facing starter prompt recipes pass product-quality screenshot review.
Each fixed starter has before/after prompt-fidelity evidence with source prompt, corrected failure mode, code path, screenshots, route health, and human verdict.
Legacy AI-runtime code is outside the active workspace.
The public authoring model is source code plus typed assets.
The active starter-template directory contains only the three starter templates.
The three starter templates install, build, render, preview, and recover from common asset errors in clean directories.
Held-back template experiments are outside the active starter-template directory and documented in archive.
Active apps directories are classified.
Marketing speaks in product and workflow language.
Public site checks reject draft-copy, internal-status, and version-cycle wording.
Broad product confidence depends on focused release checks and dogfood, not aggregate monorepo test counts.
Extra apps routes are evidence and not the primary getting-started path.
Bundle-size proof measures built bundles, including starter apps.

knownGaps.gap: The broader effects/VFX/postprocess surface is contact-sheet proven only at starter/helper level.
knownGaps.nextAction: Keep check:effects-vfx passing, then add route-level screenshots and human review before marketing particle presets, cinematic approximations, or compatibility VFX as premium production VFX.

knownGaps.gap: Broad prompt-to-visual product quality beyond approved starter recipes is not fully proven.
knownGaps.nextAction: Keep the starter product-quality screenshots under regression review, then add more positive prompt fixtures, broader asset coverage, repair-loop evidence, and external agent/user dogfood before claiming broad arbitrary prompt-to-visual quality.

knownGaps.gap: Cursor and Copilot context-only agent runs are not complete.
knownGaps.nextAction: Codex five-task local evidence and Claude Code external-agent evidence now pass. Run the same five-task context-only script against subscribed Cursor and Copilot environments.

knownGaps.gap: Licensed wild-asset corpus is not broad enough.
knownGaps.nextAction: The asset corpus now covers generated/adversarial assets, pinned Khronos/product-form/material-extension/Blender-export/animation/textured-PBR/KTX2 fixtures, downloaded Poly Haven CC0 glTF, downloaded Khronos Draco-compressed glTF, and an authenticated Sketchfab CC0 GLB with browser render proof. Add Meshy exports with source/license notes, then run add/validate/typegen/render.

knownGaps.gap: Real external deployment smoke is not complete across Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, and Netlify.
knownGaps.nextAction: Vercel and Cloudflare Pages public smoke now render WebGL2 Aura3D canvases from deployed product-viewer artifacts. Provide Netlify credentials or a project target, then record public URL, route health, screenshot, MIME checks, and deployment-check output for that host.

knownGaps.gap: Marketing comprehension interviews are not complete.
knownGaps.nextAction: Show the marketing site to an indie React developer, a Three.js-experienced 3D artist, and a non-technical product manager, then record answers to the comprehension rubric.

knownGaps.gap: Outside beta dogfood is not complete.
knownGaps.nextAction: Publish beta artifacts, recruit at least five external install/scaffold attempts, record feedback in issues or dogfood docs, and fix or document critical bugs.
